provenance.source_corpus: Effect of Flea Beetles (Aphthona Nigriscutis) on Prairie Invaded by Leafy Spurge (Euphorbia Esula) in Maintoba

source_spans.text: Aphthona nigriscutis, the black dot flea beetle, was released as a biological control agent in nine heavily infested sites in south-central Manitoba
Leafy spurge near the release points showed significantly lower cover, biomass, height, and stem density than at the reference points.
Leafy spurge near the release points showed significantly lower cover, biomass, height, and stem density than at the reference points.
Leafy spurge near the release points showed significantly lower cover, biomass, height, and stem density than at the reference points.
Leafy spurge near the release points showed significantly lower cover, biomass, height, and stem density than at the reference points.
Also, release points had significantly more bare ground, higher grass and sedge biomass, and greater plant diversity.
Also, release points had significantly more bare ground, higher grass and sedge biomass, and greater plant diversity.
Also, release points had significantly more bare ground, higher grass and sedge biomass, and greater plant diversity.
Both litter frequency and cover were lower near the release point than at the paired nonrelease reference point
